last_digest.title: CISA gives feds 3 days to patch Check Point VPN bug exploited as zero-day
last_digest.rss_summary: CISA has ordered U.S. government agencies to secure their Check Point Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access deployments against a critical vulnerability exploited in zero-day attacks by Qilin ransomware affiliates. [...]

last_digest.title: New Shai-Hulud attack trojanizes 19 science-focused PyPI packages
last_digest.rss_summary: Hackers compromised 19 packages on the PyPI, collectively downloaded hundreds of thousands of times, in a new Shai-Hulud supply-chain attack that delivered malware designed to steal developer secrets. [...]

last_digest.title: Meta Accuses NSO of Violating WhatsApp Court Injunction
last_digest.rss_summary: Meta says NSO violated a court injunction by targeting WhatsApp users again through phishing campaigns and test accounts. Last year, WhatsApp won a landmark case against NSO Group, the Israeli spyware vendor behind Pegasus, and secured a permanent court injunction barring the company from ever targeting WhatsApp or its users again. The court was unambiguous: […]

last_digest.title: 174,000 Impacted by Lansing Community College Data Breach
last_digest.rss_summary:
Hackers accessed personal information stored on certain Lansing Community College systems in February 2025.
The post 174,000 Impacted by Lansing Community College Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

last_digest.title: Ransomware sends Illinois high school on an early summer vacation
last_digest.rss_summary: Meanwhile, 13 schools in Wales affected by separate attack

last_week_digest.title: Meet GREYVIBE, the Russia-Linked Hacking Group Using AI to Target Ukraine and Still Making Rookie Mistakes
last_week_digest.tldr: The Russia-linked group GREYVIBE has launched five distinct attack chains against Ukrainian military, government, and commercial entities since August 2025. By integrating AI tools like ChatGPT and Ideogram to generate malware and infrastructure, the group compensates for operational inexperience. This trend signals an evolving threat landscape where lower-skilled actors leverage artificial intelligence to execute persistent, complex campaigns, effectively blurring the lines between state-sponsored espionage and cybercriminal activity.

last_week_digest.title: ShinyHunters Leaks Charter Communications Data, Potentially Impacting 5 Million Customers
last_week_digest.tldr: The cybercriminal group ShinyHunters leaked data stolen from Charter Communications after a failed extortion attempt, exposing approximately 4.9 million customer records, including names, email addresses, and phone numbers. While the company confirmed the breach, it denied the theft of sensitive proprietary network information. This incident underscores the persistent threat of extortion-driven data leaks, signalling that large telecommunications providers remain primary targets for groups leveraging stolen credentials.

last_week_digest.title: Palo Alto GlobalProtect VPN auth bypass flaw now exploited in attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Palo Alto Networks confirmed active exploitation of a critical authentication bypass vulnerability, CVE-2026-0257, within its GlobalProtect VPN software. Attackers are leveraging forged cookies to gain unauthorised network access, prompting CISA to mandate urgent patching. This incident highlights significant operational risks for global enterprises relying on VPN gateways, signalling that attackers are increasingly targeting identity-based authentication mechanisms to circumvent traditional perimeter defences and compromise internal corporate infrastructure at scale.

last_week_digest.title: 23andMe inherits lawsuit over 'disturbing' DNA data breach
last_week_digest.tldr: California regulators are suing 23andMe following a 2023 breach that exposed the sensitive genetic data of nearly seven million users. Although the initial intrusion targeted only 14,000 accounts, systemic security failures allowed widespread unauthorised access. This litigation highlights the severe operational risks of mishandling highly personal information, signalling that regulators are increasingly prioritising corporate accountability and transparency regarding incident response protocols for firms managing sensitive global consumer datasets.

last_week_digest.title: In Other News: Trump Mobile Data Breach, FIFA World Cup Phishing, CISA Responds to Supply Chain Attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Trump Mobile recently suffered a data breach exposing sensitive customer information, highlighting persistent vulnerabilities in telecommunications infrastructure. Simultaneously, malicious actors are leveraging the 2026 FIFA World Cup to launch sophisticated phishing campaigns, while CISA addresses mounting risks within global supply chains. These developments signal a critical shift, as attackers increasingly exploit high-profile events and systemic third-party dependencies, forcing organisations to prioritise robust data protection and proactive threat intelligence.

last_week_digest.title: Attackers Abuse Shared Content for ChatGPT Phishing Campaign
last_week_digest.tldr: Threat actors are exploiting legitimate ChatGPT and Claude shared content features to distribute infostealer malware through sophisticated ClickFix campaigns. By leveraging trusted domains and conditional rendering to bypass automated security scanners, attackers increasingly use malvertising and SEO poisoning to target unsuspecting users. This shift signals a broader industry trend where adversaries weaponise AI-normalised installation workflows, forcing organisations to rethink security awareness regarding command-line execution and browser-based threats.

last_week_digest.title: Hackers hijack thousands of sites for ClickFix and FakeUpdate attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: The DriveSurge threat actor has compromised thousands of high-reputation websites to facilitate large-scale malware distribution via ClickFix and FakeUpdate social engineering tactics. By acting as an initial access broker, the group redirects unsuspecting visitors to malicious payloads, threatening both Windows and macOS environments. This campaign highlights a growing industry trend where attackers weaponise legitimate infrastructure, forcing organisations to reconsider the security of their digital supply chains globally.

last_week_digest.title: Afghan finance officials targeted by suspected Pakistani cyberespionage campaign
last_week_digest.tldr: The Pakistan-linked threat actor SideCopy has launched a sophisticated cyberespionage campaign targeting Afghanistan’s Ministry of Finance and provincial officials. By leveraging compromised government infrastructure to distribute XenoRAT malware via targeted phishing, attackers gained persistent access to sensitive state communications. This operation highlights a growing trend of regional adversaries utilising highly tailored reconnaissance to bypass network defences, signalling an urgent need for enhanced state-level email security protocols globally.

last_week_digest.title: Red Hat npm packages compromised to steal developer credentials
last_week_digest.tldr: Attackers compromised 32 Red Hat npm packages, exposing 117,000 weekly downloads to the Miasma credential-stealing malware. By hijacking a developer’s GitHub account, threat actors injected malicious scripts to harvest cloud secrets, SSH keys, and CI/CD tokens. While Red Hat reports no impact on customer production systems, this incident highlights the persistent vulnerability of software supply chains, signalling that trusted developer ecosystems remain primary targets for sophisticated credential theft.

last_week_digest.title: Hackers duped Meta AI support chatbot to steal celebrity Instagram accounts
last_week_digest.tldr: Hackers exploited Meta’s AI support chatbot to hijack thousands of Instagram accounts by manipulating email recovery processes, resulting in the theft of high-value handles worth over $1 million. This incident highlights the severe operational risks of integrating large language models with elevated system permissions. It signals a critical industry shift, demonstrating that deploying AI agents without robust, deterministic security guardrails creates dangerous vulnerabilities for global platform integrity.

last_week_digest.title: China Uses Dual-Method Cyberattack on Czech Orgs
last_week_digest.tldr: Chinese threat actors are targeting Czech and Taiwanese government, academic, and financial sectors through the Operation Dragon Weave spear-phishing campaign. Utilizing dual-method Azureveil malware, these attacks facilitate sensitive data exfiltration. This trend signals that geopolitical tensions are increasingly driving sophisticated, multi-stage cyber espionage against high-value European and Asian institutional targets.

last_week_digest.title: Russia claims foreign spy agencies hacked officials' phones
last_week_digest.tldr: Russia’s Federal Security Service alleges foreign intelligence agencies conducted a large-scale espionage operation, compromising mobile devices of senior officials to extract sensitive communications and geolocation data. This incident signals a growing trend of state-sponsored actors weaponising global IT infrastructure, forcing international technology firms to confront increasing geopolitical scrutiny and liability.

last_week_digest.title: VS Code zero-day lets hackers steal GitHub tokens in one click
last_week_digest.tldr: A newly disclosed zero-day vulnerability in Visual Studio Code allows attackers to steal GitHub OAuth tokens via malicious extensions, granting unauthorised access to private repositories. This flaw highlights critical supply chain risks for developers and signals growing industry frustration regarding Microsoft’s handling of security disclosures and its opaque patch management processes.

last_week_digest.title: Over 116,000 Minecraft systems infected in WeedHack malware campaign
last_week_digest.tldr: The WeedHack malware campaign has compromised over 116,000 Minecraft systems globally since January via malicious mods and SEO poisoning. This Malware-as-a-Service operation steals credentials and financial data, signalling a growing trend where accessible, low-cost infostealers empower amateur actors to conduct large-scale cyberattacks, significantly increasing risks for individual users and digital platforms.

last_week_digest.title: Supply Chain Attack Hits 32 Red Hat NPM Packages
last_week_digest.tldr: Hackers recently compromised 32 Red Hat NPM packages, publishing 96 malicious versions containing credential-stealing malware. This supply chain breach threatens global software integrity, forcing organisations to urgently audit dependencies. The incident signals a growing industry trend where attackers exploit open-source ecosystems to infiltrate corporate environments, necessitating more rigorous automated security vetting.

last_week_digest.title: Malware campaign targeting Minecraft users infects over 116,000 systems
last_week_digest.tldr: The WeedHack malware campaign has infected over 116,000 systems globally, leveraging SEO poisoning and malicious Minecraft mods to harvest credentials and provide remote access. This low-cost MaaS operation signals a dangerous shift toward accessible, user-friendly cybercrime tools that threaten both individual users and corporate security.

last_week_digest.title: CISA warns of cyberattacks targeting fuel tank monitoring systems
last_week_digest.tldr: Hackers are compromising internet-exposed automatic tank gauge systems across critical infrastructure, enabling unauthorised control over fuel monitoring and safety alerts. This vulnerability signals an urgent need for global industrial sectors to secure remote access, as attackers increasingly target operational technology to disrupt essential supply chains.

last_week_digest.title: Cyber espionage campaign targeted stock exchange executive’s Outlook account
last_week_digest.tldr: Attackers maintained 150 days of silent access to a global stock exchange executive’s Outlook account, exfiltrating sensitive market intelligence via legitimate cloud services. This espionage operation highlights how targeted, low-profile compromises of senior leadership can bypass traditional network defences to expose critical, non-public corporate data.

last_week_digest.title: Security of 100 AI Agents Tested and Ranked – What You Need to Know
last_week_digest.tldr: A comprehensive assessment of 100 AI agents reveals significant security vulnerabilities, highlighting the urgent need for robust defensive frameworks. As enterprises integrate these tools, the potential for operational compromise signals a critical industry shift towards prioritising rigorous risk management and standardised security evaluation protocols globally.

last_week_digest.title: Nobody needs Mythos or 0-days to build a chaos-causing computer worm – free open source models work just fine
last_week_digest.tldr: University of Toronto researchers demonstrated that free, open-source AI models can autonomously propagate worms through enterprise networks by exploiting known vulnerabilities. This development signals a shift where attackers cheaply operationalise common security flaws at scale, significantly reducing the window for organisations to remediate human errors.

last_week_digest.title: Russia seeks to label two anti-Kremlin hacker groups as ‘extremist’
last_week_digest.tldr: Russia is moving to designate hacker groups Belarusian Cyber Partisans and Silent Crow as extremist organisations following high-profile attacks, including a 2025 Aeroflot breach that cancelled over 100 flights for 20,000 passengers. This legal escalation signals a hardening stance against hacktivism, highlighting the growing operational risks critical infrastructure faces from politically motivated cyber warfare.

last_week_digest.title: Chinese Cybercrime Group in Spotlight for Record Campaign Pace
last_week_digest.tldr: A prolific Chinese cybercrime syndicate is accelerating its operational tempo, leveraging sophisticated social engineering, credential phishing, and malware distribution to compromise global organisations. This surge in activity threatens significant financial losses and operational disruption for businesses worldwide. Such rapid campaign scaling signals a dangerous shift, forcing international firms to overhaul their defensive security postures immediately.

last_week_digest.title: Cisco warns of critical Unified CM flaw with PoC exploit code
last_week_digest.tldr: Cisco has released critical patches for a Unified Communications Manager vulnerability, CVE-2026-20230, which allows unprivileged remote attackers to gain root access. While no active exploitation is currently confirmed, the existence of public proof-of-concept code signals a heightened risk to global telephony infrastructure, necessitating immediate updates or service disabling to prevent potential system-wide compromise.

last_week_digest.title: Chinese hackers use new Atlas RAT malware in European cyberattacks
last_week_digest.tldr: The Chinese-speaking threat actor TA4922 is aggressively targeting European organisations, including those in Germany, Italy, and the UK, using the new Atlas RAT and RomulusLoader. By deploying diverse phishing lures and AI-assisted malware, this group signals a shift toward high-tempo, financially motivated cybercrime that creates significant risks of data theft and corporate surveillance.

last_week_digest.title: Gamaredon Uses WinRAR Vulnerability to Launch Modular Spy Campaign on Ukrainian Targets
last_week_digest.tldr: The Russia-linked group Gamaredon is exploiting a critical WinRAR vulnerability to deploy modular, evasive malware against Ukrainian targets. By leveraging path traversal to achieve persistence, the campaign compromises operational security and facilitates data theft. This trend signals that state-sponsored actors are increasingly adopting sophisticated, fragmented toolsets to maintain long-term access within critical infrastructure.

last_week_digest.title: DentaQuest Breach: ShinyHunters Publish Data Impacting 2.6M People
last_week_digest.tldr: The extortion group ShinyHunters leaked 234 gigabytes of sensitive data from dental administrator DentaQuest after failed ransom negotiations. This breach exposed personal information and Medicaid IDs for 2.6 million individuals. The incident highlights the persistent threat of extortion campaigns against healthcare providers, signalling that organisations must prioritise robust data protection against criminal syndicates.

last_week_digest.title: Chinese APT deploys new malware to keep access to hacked networks
last_week_digest.tldr: The Chinese threat group UNC5221 maintained undetected access to victim networks for over 18 months by compromising managed service providers and deploying advanced backdoors like Brickstorm and Plenet. This breach highlights the critical risk of supply chain vulnerabilities, signalling that sophisticated actors are increasingly targeting service providers to bypass security and persist across global infrastructures.

last_week_digest.title: Silent Ransom Group (SRG): Switching To DNS Fast Flux Infrastructure
last_week_digest.tldr: The Silent Ransom Group has adopted resilient Fast Flux infrastructure, leveraging compromised IoT devices globally to facilitate data extortion against law firms and financial institutions. This shift signals a sophisticated evolution in cybercrime, forcing organisations to recognise that botnet-driven evasion tactics now pose a significant, persistent threat to international business and data integrity.

last_week_digest.title: Hackers Leak DentaQuest Information Impacting 2.6 Million
last_week_digest.tldr: The ShinyHunters extortion group leaked 234 gigabytes of sensitive data stolen from dental benefits administrator DentaQuest, exposing personal information of 2.6 million individuals. This significant breach highlights the escalating operational risks facing healthcare providers globally, signalling that cybercriminals are increasingly targeting massive administrative datasets to leverage extortion against organisations and compromise millions of global patients.

last_week_digest.title: Oxford Uni student data pwned yet again - this time via career platform breach
last_week_digest.tldr: Oxford University students and staff face renewed security concerns after a breach of the CareerConnect platform by provider Group GTI. This incident, following a separate massive compromise of the Canvas system, exposes names, emails, and encrypted passwords. These repeated failures signal a critical need for universities to rigorously audit third-party vendors managing sensitive data.

last_week_digest.title: CISA gives feds 3 days to patch Check Point VPN bug exploited as zero-day
last_week_digest.tldr: CISA has mandated that federal agencies patch a critical Check Point VPN vulnerability within three days after Qilin ransomware affiliates exploited the flaw. Impacting dozens of global organisations, this incident highlights the persistent risk legacy protocols pose to enterprise security and critical infrastructure worldwide.

last_week_digest.title: New Shai-Hulud attack trojanizes 19 science-focused PyPI packages
last_week_digest.tldr: The Shai-Hulud campaign compromised 19 science-focused PyPI packages, impacting hundreds of thousands of downloads. By injecting malicious code to harvest developer secrets and cloud credentials, the attack highlights critical supply-chain vulnerabilities, signalling an urgent need for rigorous dependency vetting within global software development workflows.

last_week_digest.title: Meta Accuses NSO of Violating WhatsApp Court Injunction
last_week_digest.tldr: Meta has filed a contempt motion against NSO Group for violating a permanent injunction by launching new phishing campaigns against WhatsApp users. This defiance highlights the persistent threat posed by commercial spyware vendors, signalling that global digital platforms face escalating challenges in protecting billions of users.

last_week_digest.title: 174,000 Impacted by Lansing Community College Data Breach
last_week_digest.tldr: Lansing Community College recently confirmed a significant data breach affecting 174,000 individuals after hackers accessed internal systems in February 2025. This incident highlights the growing vulnerability of educational institutions, signalling that global organisations must urgently prioritise robust data protection to mitigate operational risks.

last_week_digest.title: Ransomware sends Illinois high school on an early summer vacation
last_week_digest.tldr: Ransomware attacks in Illinois and Wales have disrupted educational operations, forcing school closures and compromising sensitive personal data. These incidents highlight the education sector’s growing vulnerability to cybercriminals, signalling an urgent need for robust security infrastructure to protect institutional networks and critical student information globally.
